title: Transcrptome analysis via photo-isolation chemistry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"t1jmk","text":"To gain insights into tissue-specific gene expression in multicellular systems, gene expression profiles are required to be precisely linked with spatial information. Here, we establish a hight-depth spatial transcriptomics method, photo-isolation chemistrty (PIC), which is able to isolate gene expression profiles only from UV-irradiatied region out of whole tissue section. This method performs reverse transcription on tissue sections using photocaged oligo DNAs. After the UV irradiation, the cDNAs in the irradiated regions are allowed to be amplified and sequenced, thereby providing gene expression profiles linked with spatial information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yscl2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

No. 638324)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5h8jc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Crush dry ice into pieces of 2-3 cm in size
Put the crushed dry ice lumps into a 100 ml beaker, then fill it with iso-pentane
Put the dissected tissues into an embedding container filled with O.C.T compound
Flash-freeze the tissues by submerging into the iso-pentane cooled with dry ice.

Make the sections at a thickness of 10 μm with a cryostat and mount them on MAS-coated glass slides (Matsunami). Note: Subsequent processing can be facilitated by creating the hydrophobic barriers around the sections with a pap pen facilitates
Wash the tissue sections twice with PBS to remove the O.C.T compound
Fix the sections with 4% PFA in PBS for 10 min at room temperature
Wash the sections twice with PBS
Incubate the sections for 3 min with PBS containing 3% Triton X-100 at room temperature
Wash the sections twice with PBS
Incubate the sections with 0.1N HCl in nuclease-free water for 5 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Incubate the sections with 1M Tris-HCl (pH8.0) for 5 min at room temperature
Wash the sections twice with PBS
Immerse the sections in PBS preheated to 65°C in a staining jar and incubate in a hybridization oven for 5min
Immerse the sections in a staining jar containing chilled PBS
Mix 0.5 μl of 500 ng/μl caged Oligo DNA, 0.5 μl of 10 mM dNTPs, and 5 μl of nuclease-free water in a 0.2 ml tube, and heat to 65°C and then quickly cool to 4°C using Thermal cycler
Add 2 μl of 5× First Strand Buffer, 1 μl of 0.1 M DTT, 0.5 μl of 40 U/μl RNase Out, and 0.5 μl of 200 U/μl Superscript II Reverse Transcriptase to the primer mix prepared above (17)
Drop 10 μl of the RT mix prepared above (18) onto the section and seal with a coverslip
Incubate the sections at 42 °C for 1 hr in a PBS-humidified chamber
Immerse the sections in PBS preheated to 70 °C in a staining jar and keep at 70 °C in a hybridization oven for 10min
Immerse the sections in a staining jar cold containing chilled PBS
Wash the sections twice with PBS
Incubate the sections for 15 min with blocking solution at room temperature
Incubate the sections with primary antibodies diluted in blocking solution for 2 hr at room temperature or overnight at 4 °C in a hybridization oven
Wash the sections three times with TBST
Incubate the sections with 2nd antibodies diluted in blocking solution for 1 hr at room temperature or overnight at 4 °C in a hybridization oven
Wash the sections three times with TBST
Incubate the sections with Nuclear Violet LCS (1:1000) with blocking solution for 15 min at room temperature in a hybridization oven
Mount the sections with SlowFade Diamond before coverslipping

Irradiate 365 nm-centered UV light to the ROIs for 15 min Note: Since the caged compound (NPOM) used in PIC is cleaved most efficiently at 365 nm, the irradiation can be performed with the fluorescence filter cube that are conventionally used to excite nuclear staining dyes with blue fluorescence such as DAPI and Hoechst. We used Leica A filter cube (#11513873; 340–380 nm excitation) for uncaging under a Leica DM5000 B fluorescence microscope illuminated with an EL6000 100 W Hg lamp.
Strip the cover slip, and wash the sections three times with PBS
Drop 40 μl of proteinase K lysis solution to the sections
Incubate the sections in a hybridization oven at 42 °C for 30 min
Scrape the sections and collect the cell lysates and debris in a 1.5 ml tube
Drop 40 μl of proteinase K lysis solution to the section, again
Incubate the sections in a hybridization oven at 42 °C for 30 min, again
Collect the cell lysates and debris in the same tube as (36) (total volume ≃ 80 μl)
Incubate for 30 min at 55 °C
Purify cDNA:mRNA hybrids with Qiagen MinElute PCR Purification kit
Elute with 15 μl of nuclease-free water

Mix the eluted 15 μl of cDNA:mRNA hybrids with 2 μl of 5xFirst Strand Buffer, 2.31 μl of 5x Second Strand Buffer, 0.23 μl of 10 mM dNTPs, 0.08 μl of E.coli DNA ligase (10 U/μl), 0.3 μl of E.coli DNA polumerase (10 U/μl) and 0.08 μl of E.coli RNaseH (2 U/μl)
Incubate for 2 hr at 16 °C
Mix 20 μl of second-strand DNA sample and 24 μl of Ampure binding buffer mix (Ampure XP beads : Beads binding buffer = 1 : 5) and vortex thoroughly
Incubate for 15 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 80% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 80% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Spin down the sample tube
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 2 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant and air dry
Suspend the beads with 6.4 μl of nuclease-free water by pipetting

Mix the 6.4 μl of beads suspension containing double-strand cDNAs with 1.6 μl of ATP, 1.6 μl of GTP, 1.6 μl of CTP, 1.6 μl of UTP, 1.6 μl of 10xT7 reaction buffer, and 1.6 μl of T7 Enzyme from the MEGAscript T7 Transcription Kit
Incubate for 17 hr at 37 °C
Add 1 μl of TURBO DNase from the MEGAscript T7 Transcription Kit and incubate for 15 min at 37 °C
Add 3 μl of ExoSAP-IT Express PCR Product Cleanup and incubate for 5 min at 37 °C
Add 5.5 μl of fragmentation Buffer and incubate for 3 min at 94 °C and quickly cool to 4°C using Thermal cycler
Place the sample tube on ice and add 2.75 μl of 0.5 M EDTA pH8.0
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Collect 28 μl of supernatant in a new 0.2 ml tube
Mix 28 μl of aRNA samples with 50.4 μl of RNAClean XP beads and vortex thoroughly
Incubate for 10 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 70% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 70% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Spin down the sample tube
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 2 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant and air dry
Suspend the beads with 7 μl of nuclease-free water by pipetting
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Collect 5 μl of supernatant in a new 0.2 ml tube
Mix eluted 5 μl of aRNAs with 1 μl of RandomhexRT (250 ng) and 0.5 μl of 10 mM dNTPs
Heat the tubes at 65°C and then quickly cool to 4°C using Thermal cycler
Add 2 μl of 5× First Strand Buffer, 1 μl of 0.1 M DTT, 0.5 μl of 40 U/μl RNase Out, and 0.5 μl of 200 U/μl Superscript II Reverse Transcriptase
Incubate for 10 min at 25°C, then for 1 hr at 42°C
Add 8.4 μl of nuclease-free water, 1.8 μl of RNA PCR primer 1 (10 μM), 1.8 μl of RNA PCR primer 2 (10 μM) and 22.5 μl of Pusion High-Fidelity PCR Master Mix
Amplify by PCR (98°C for 30 s, followed by 11 cycles of 98°C for 10 s, 60°C for 30 s and 72°C for 30 s, with final extension at 72°C for 10 min)
Add 1 μl of RNaseA (10 mg/ml) and incubate for 30 min at 37 °C
Add 6 μl of nuclease-free water and 32.5 μl Ampure XP beads, and vortex thoroughly
Incubate for 15 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Collect the supernatant in a new 0.2 ml tube
Add 12.5 μl Ampure XP beads, and vortex thoroughly
Incubate for 10 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 80% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 80% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Spin down the sample tube
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 2 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant and air dry
Suspend the beads with 32 μl of nuclease-free water by pipetting
Incubate for 2 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Collect 30 μl of supernatant in a new 0.2 ml tube
Add 19.5 μl Ampure XP beads, and vortex thoroughly
Incubate for 15 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Collect the supernatant in a new 0.2 ml tube
Add 7.5 μl Ampure XP beads, and vortex thoroughly
Incubate for 10 min at room temperature
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 80% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Add 200 μl of 80% Ethanol and incubate for 30 sec at room temperature
Remove the supernatant
Spin down the sample tube
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 2 min at room temperature
Remove the supernatant and air dry
Suspend the beads with 17.5 μl of nuclease-free water by pipetting
Place the sample tube on the magnetic stand and incubate for 5 min at room temperature
Collect 15 μl of supernatant in a new 0.2 ml tube
Quantify cDNA library using high-sensitivity DNA chips on a Bioanalyzer 2100 and Library Quantification Kit
Sequence with Illumina sequencer (1–3 million reads per sample)
Analyze the sequence data according to CEL-seq2 protocol (Hashimshony, T. et al. Genome Biol 2016)
creator: Protocols Importer
